symptoms.symptom_name: Delayed severe post-exertional malaise (PEM) after minor exertion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A delayed, disproportionate crash after minor exertion is the defining feature of PEM/ME/CFS.

symptoms.symptom_name: Partner-assisted FAST negative (no droop, no unilateral weakness, no slurred speech)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative FAST with symmetric findings argues strongly against acute stroke, though it is not a substitute for full assessment if focal features emerge.

symptoms.symptom_name: Global symmetric cognitive slowing (not sudden focal deficit)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Global slowing/word-finding as part of a stereotyped crash fits PEM rather than focal stroke.
Global symmetric slowing is against a focal vascular lesion.
Afebrile with normal breathing and rousability argues against sepsis/encephalopathy.
Sensory hypersensitivity is characteristic of severe crashes.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profoundly unrefreshing prolonged sleep (14h, no recovery)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Unrefreshing sleep is a core ME/CFS feature.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intact cranial nerves/reflexes, no focal deficit; tender cervical nodes (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No focal deficit with tender nodes fits a post-viral/immune state rather than stroke.
Absence of focal deficits on exam argues against stroke.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known ME/CFS / post-COVID with prior stereotyped crashes

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A consistent prior crash pattern strongly supports PEM over a new acute event.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A delayed severe crash after minor exertion, with a negative partner-assisted FAST, global symmetric slowing, afebrile and rousable, in a patient with prior stereotyped crashes, is a severe PEM episode — not stroke or sepsis. Management is radical rest and supportive care; an ED transfer risks worsening the crash and should be reserved for genuine emergency features.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Partner-assisted FAST/stroke screen (repeat if any change)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The key remote step to exclude acute stroke; positive features mandate 000.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Basic bloods (FBC, CRP, electrolytes, glucose) via a low-exertion pathway if indicated
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude infective/metabolic contributors without provoking a crash; consider home/deferred phlebotomy.
Only if focal features or genuine stroke concern arise; routine imaging is not indicated for a stereotyped PEM crash and the trip may harm.
